title: Crypto-D7 -- CODR-1 closeout + next-hypothesis selection memo (memo only)

crypto_d4_spec.what: Froze CODR-1 v1 (Crypto Oversold-Dip Reversion in Uptrend): long-only spot; close_t>SMA200 AND ret_t<=-7% -> enter open_{t+1}; one position/symbol, no pyramiding; -10% close stop (precedence) else time stop at close of 5th held bar; no profit target. Pre-registered, no parameter freedom.

crypto_d5_engine_tests.what: Implemented the frozen engine (engine/crypto_codr1.py, stdlib-only, offline, GROSS returns, friction as separate layer) + tests. No run.

crypto_d6_is_baseline.what: Ran CODR-1 IS-only on frozen BTC/ETH/SOL spot daily (BTC/ETH 2020-2023, SOL partial 2020-08-11..2023). SMA200 warmed inside IS; 0 rows from 2024/2025/2026 used. Verdict IS_FAIL.

2_codr1_final_verdict.note: Per the factory ladder, IS_FAIL stops the branch BEFORE any OOS pre-registration. The D2 future-sequence had labeled D7 'OOS protocol pre-registration', but CODR-1 never earned OOS, so D7 is repurposed as a closeout + idea-selection memo. OOS 2024-2025 and 2026 remain SEALED and untouched.

3_main_failure_reasons.btc_primary_hard_fail: BTC (the designated primary) lost money after costs: 9 trades, net@0.30% total -4.93% (avg -0.55%/trade), gross PF 0.911, net PF 0.81. A strategy whose primary symbol is unprofitable cannot advance.
3_main_failure_reasons.btc_trade_count_too_low: BTC produced only 9 IS trades vs the >=30/symbol gate -- far too thin for any statistical claim; ETH (27) was also below 30.
3_main_failure_reasons.net_pf_below_threshold: Net profit factor after the pre-registered 0.30% round-trip friction was below 1.3 on BTC (0.81), ETH (1.21) and the combined pool (1.28); only SOL cleared 1.3 (1.45).
3_main_failure_reasons.top3_gate_failed_under_stress: The HARD top-3-winner-removal gate failed on all three symbols at net@0.30% (BTC -25.12%, ETH -35.05%, SOL -2.86% ex-top-3). The combined pool passed at net@0.30% (+6.74%) but FAILED under +50% friction stress (net@0.45% ex-top-3 = -2.71%).
3_main_failure_reasons.edge_concentrated_in_2020_2021_bull: The close>SMA200 trend filter suppresses entries in the 2022 bear; combined trades collapse to 2 (2022) and 5 (2023). Net P&L is overwhelmingly 2020-2021. The strategy is effectively a bull-only dip-buyer -- exactly the regime fragility the ladder is built to catch.
3_main_failure_reasons.combined_pass_was_pooling_dependent: The only gates the combined pool cleared (top-3 at base friction) were cleared by pooling 66 trades across three coins, which dilutes any single coin's winner concentration. It does not represent per-symbol robust evidence and cannot rescue a primary-symbol failure.

4_what_this_teaches.simple_oversold_dip_not_enough: A single -7% down-day inside an uptrend is too weak/too rare a causal signal on daily spot: it neither fires often enough (BTC 9 trades) nor produces an edge robust to friction and winner-removal.
4_what_this_teaches.btc_primary_must_carry_evidence: Multi-asset corroboration is a SECONDARY check, not a substitute. If BTC (the deepest, most-liquid market and designated primary) cannot carry its own positive, friction-surviving, winner-robust evidence, the hypothesis fails regardless of ETH/SOL.
4_what_this_teaches.pooling_cannot_hide_weak_symbol_level_ev: Pooling improves apparent trade count and dilutes winner concentration, but it masks per-symbol fragility. Future gates must be read per-symbol first, pooled second.
4_what_this_teaches.top_winner_gate_remains_crucial: The top-3-winner-removal gate -- the gate that parked every futures branch -- once again exposed concentration that headline returns hid. It stays a hard, non-negotiable gate.
4_what_this_teaches.crypto_needs_more_signals_or_a_different: Daily spot BTC simply does not generate enough discrete shock events to power a thin event-driven strategy to >=30 trades with a friction-robust edge. The next hypothesis must EITHER fire materially more often (higher signal density / more frequent timeframe) OR rest on a stronger, more specific causal mechanism than 'a one-day dip in an uptrend.'

5_data_readiness_remains_useful.frozen_spot_data_still_valid: The immutable, SHA256-pinned, 2026-sealed BTC/ETH/SOL spot daily USDT snapshot (Crypto-D3b 5b3d94c, ratified D3c fe5594f; BTC d87742b8, ETH 4a4d7b08, SOL bd8b28f1) remains QA-CLEAN and ready to back the NEXT separately-authorized crypto hypothesis. CODR-1's failure is a strategy failure, not a data failure.
5_data_readiness_remains_useful.perps_blocked: Perps remain BLOCKED until funding-rate data is sourced and funding rules are frozen (Crypto-D1 4/9; D2 item #4 funding work is know-how, not authorization).
5_data_readiness_remains_useful.advisable_before_next_edge_claim: Quote reconciliation (USDT vs USD/USDC) and an independent second-source cross-check (e.g. Kraken/Coinbase) remain advisable before any future edge claim, per D3b.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Crypto volatility compression -> expansion breakout
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: D4 candidate #3, REJECTED as first pick (2-param entry adds parameter freedom).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: Low-volatility coils preceding range expansion are a documented behaviour; could fire in both bull and bear, unlike CODR-1's bull-only filter.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: A compression->expansion entry needs at least a lookback window AND a threshold -> parameter freedom that fights the no-sweep discipline; expansion captures can be fat-tailed -> top-winner risk.
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Novel vs CODR-1, but its parameter-freedom problem is exactly why D4 rejected it first.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Cross-asset momentum leadership (BTC leads ETH/SOL)
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: D4 candidate #4, REJECTED (weak paradox -- corroboration baked into the signal).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: BTC moves often lead alt moves; a lead-lag signal could be a genuine structural effect.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: Baking BTC->alt corroboration INTO the entry undercuts the independent multi-asset robustness gate (the signal and the corroboration are the same thing). Also leaves BTC itself without a primary entry -- the opposite of the D6 lesson that BTC must carry evidence.
6_next_hypothesis_options.btc_eth_sol_testability: weak paradox -- corroboration and signal collapse together
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Directly conflicts with the D6 lesson; rejected reasoning still holds.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Trend continuation after high-volume breakout (with explicit top-winner safeguards)
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: D4 candidate #1 (trend/Donchian) family + old ema_pullback (D2 item #2), which is the S26/S27 trend-continuation family ALREADY PARKED in futures.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: Volume-confirmed breakouts can ride strong crypto trends; the volume filter is a stronger causal hook than a bare price breakout.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: Trend continuation is the canonical top-winner-dependent structure -- a few fat-tail winners carry the net. 'Safeguards' (capped horizon / winner trimming) blunt the very upside the strategy relies on. This is the exact failure mode (top-winner concentration) that parked every futures branch.
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Too close to the already-PARKED trend-continuation family; structurally most likely to fail the top-3 gate.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Mean reversion after an EXTREME multi-day liquidation candle, with stricter BTC confirmation
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: A refined cousin of CODR-1 -- a STRONGER causal trigger (a forced-liquidation cascade, not a single -7% day) and an explicit BTC-carry requirement responding to the D6 lesson.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: Forced-liquidation flushes are a more specific, higher-conviction overreaction than a routine dip; requiring BTC to confirm forces the primary symbol to carry evidence directly (fixes a D6 root cause).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: Extreme liquidation events are RARER than -7% days. CODR-1 already starved BTC at 9 trades; a stricter/rarer trigger would likely produce even FEWER BTC trades and fail the >=30 gate harder. The trade-count math is the central risk.
6_next_hypothesis_options.expected_trade_count: LOW to very low (the binding risk)
6_next_hypothesis_options.top_winner_risk: lower (fixed-horizon reversion caps winners, like CODR-1)
6_next_hypothesis_options.btc_eth_sol_testability: good, and BTC-carry is built in
6_next_hypothesis_options.needs_new_data: no for daily spot; BUT may need intraday/4H or a liquidation/volume proxy to get enough events -- which IS new data
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Adjacent to CODR-1 but mechanistically distinct; the open question is whether it can ever clear the trade-count gate on daily BTC.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Calendar / weekend / day-of-week seasonality (daily spot)
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: D4 candidate #5, REJECTED as first pick (likely friction-dominated FAIL).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: High signal density (fires on a fixed schedule) -> easily clears the >=30-trade gate; trivial, no parameter freedom.
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: Per-trade edge is tiny, so the ~0.30% round-trip friction almost certainly dominates -> friction-dominated FAIL, and 24/7 crypto has weakened any classic weekend effect over 2020-2025.
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Novel vs CODR-1, but its friction-domination problem is why D4 rejected it.

6_next_hypothesis_options.name: Regime / no-trade overlay only
6_next_hypothesis_options.prior_factory_view: D4 candidate #6 EXCLUDED (an overlay is not a strategy) + old regime-HMM study (D2 item #3: holdout never closed, SOL cross-coin transfer FAILED).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_work: A robust regime filter could improve a FUTURE entry strategy (e.g. avoid the 2022 bear that hurt CODR-1).
6_next_hypothesis_options.why_it_may_fail: An overlay has no entry of its own -- it cannot be validated as a standalone strategy through the ladder. The old HMM evidence is historical-only and unproven (holdout open, SOL transfer failed).
6_next_hypothesis_options.too_close_to_codr1_or_rejected: Same exclusion as D4/S30-D1: an overlay is not a strategy; only usable as a future add-on to a validated entry.

7_recommendation.rationale: Every option carries a known structural defect that the factory has already reasoned through: A (parameter freedom), B (conflicts with the BTC-must-carry lesson), C (top-winner-dependent trend family, already parked), E (friction-dominated), F (an overlay is not a strategy). The only option aligned with the D6 lessons -- D (liquidation-cascade reversion with BTC confirmation) -- is throttled by the SAME binding constraint that just failed CODR-1: insufficient discrete signal events on daily BTC to reach the >=30-trade gate. Selecting another thin daily-spot event strategy now would most likely reproduce CODR-1's BTC trade-count failure.
7_recommendation.what_crypto_d8_should_be: A READ-ONLY signal-density / statistical-power audit (memo only -- counting candidate events, NOT backtesting) on the frozen IS data: for each surviving family (especially D, and as a sanity floor E), count how many candidate entry events it would generate on BTC/ETH/SOL in 2020-2023 IS, and determine whether daily spot can EVER produce >=30 BTC events with a friction-survivable per-event edge. If daily spot is structurally too sparse for event-driven mechanisms, D8 should also scope whether a more frequent timeframe (4H spot -- deferred in D2) or a second data source is the real prerequisite. Only after that audit should a Crypto-D9 spec freeze a single hypothesis.
7_recommendation.conditional_runner_up: If a single hypothesis MUST be picked, option D (extreme-liquidation reversion with stricter BTC confirmation) is the least-bad single pick because it both strengthens the causal mechanism and forces BTC to carry evidence -- but it should NOT be frozen until the D8 signal-density audit confirms it can clear the BTC trade-count gate. Do not freeze a spec in this memo.

9_final_line: Crypto-D7 is a closeout and idea-selection memo only; no crypto strategy is validated, paper-ready, live-ready, or authorized for execution.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Closeout + idea-selection memo only. CODR-1 v1 is PARKED after IS_FAIL (no OOS run, no D7 OOS authorized). No next hypothesis is frozen: recommendation is NO_STRATEGY_SELECTED_NEED_MORE_RESEARCH, with Crypto-D8 proposed as a read-only signal-density/power audit. Frozen BTC/ETH/SOL spot data stays valid for future research; perps remain blocked; OOS 2024-2025 and 2026 stay sealed; crypto stays a separate research lane; S30 stays PARKED after IS_FAIL and the futures branches are untouched.
